title: xanthoxalis brittoniae
intro: Xanthoxalis brittoniae, also known as Yellow Wood Sorrel, is a perennial plant native to the eastern United States. It is a member of the Oxalis family and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's leaves and stems have been traditionally used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and soothing digestive discomfort. Xanthoxalis brittoniae is also used in herbal medicine to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
